title: Fence-aware findings section stripping
goal: Teach stripFindingsSections to recognise ```/~~~ fenced code blocks and ignore headings inside them, so reports containing findings-like lines in a code fence are no longer silently truncated, with a test covering the fenced-block case.

steps.title: Track fence state in stripFindingsSections
steps.detail: In src/state/runReport.ts detect fence lines (^\s*(```+|~~~+)); remember the opening fence char and length and close only on a same-char line of length >= the opener. Inside a fence, ignore ATX headings (neither start nor end a skip). Fixes both directions of the finding.

steps.title: Translate touched JSDoc/comments to English
steps.detail: Translate the function's Czech JSDoc and inline comments (plus new fence-logic comments) to English per the project English rule, since the function is being edited anyway.

steps.title: Add fenced-code-block tests (both directions)
steps.detail: In describe('stripFindingsSections') add: (a) a '## Adversarial findings' line inside a ```md block is kept, including prose after the closing fence; (b) a real stale findings section that contains a fenced block with a ##/# line is removed entirely with nothing leaking.

steps.title: Verify build, typecheck and tests pass
steps.detail: npm run typecheck + npm run build + full test suite green.
